titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/284

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=744 status=live modified=2021-04-07 09:08:09.564699+00 | translation moa=745 status=live modified=2021-04-07 09:08:06.271238+00

p.cl: Omnibus sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris . Walterus De Ridale filius patricii De Ridale . Salutem .

Sciatis me concessisse et hac
mea Carta confirmasse Deo . et Ecclesie Sancte Marie De Melros et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus pro animabus dominorum meorum
Dauid . Melcolmi . et Willelmi Regum scottorum et pro anima patris mei et Omnium antecessorum meorum et pro Salute Domini mei Alexandri Regis scottorum pro
salute mea et Omnium successorum meorum donationem . P . patris mei quam eisdem monachis fecit de quadam parte terre sue in territorio De Lil
lisclyue In Liberam . puram . et perpetuam elemosinam que continetur infra has diuisas . Scilicet a uado Curlewudeburne ex occidentali
parte De Caldelawe . per uiam que uadit De Selekirk’ uersus Iedewurth usque in alne . et sic descendendo per eandem aquam que est diuisa inter
terram de lilliesclyue . et terram episcopi de Glasgu . usque in sicum qui est diuisa inter prefatam terram de lillesclyue et abbatem de kelkov . et sic per di
uisas inter prenominatam terram de lillisclyue et eundem abbatem usque in Curlewudeburne et sic sursum per eandem burnam usque ad pre
dictum vadum de Curlewudeburne . Totam hanc terram et quicquid continetur infra has diuisas excepta terra quam Willelmus filius
Alexandri tenet in eodem territorio . concessi predictis Monachis de Melros . Et uolo et concedo ut ea habeant teneant et possideant inperpetuum . Ita libere .
quiete . plene . et honorifice sicut aliquam elemosinam Liberius . Quiecius . plenius . et honorificencius tenent . et possident . Insuper concessi eisdem Monachis pasturam ad duodecim boues . et decem vaccas et quinque equos . et centum oues . cum omnibus aliis communibus aesiamentis eiusdem
uille . Sicut Carta donacionis patris mei plenius proportat et testatur . Et ego et heredes mei manutenebimus et warantizabimus predictis
Monachis omnia predicta erga omnes homines et adquietabimus erga dominum regem et omnes alios de omni terreno seruicio et consuetudine et exac
tione seculari Inperpetuum .

Test’ . Comite patricio . Patricio filio eius . Roberto de Ros . Roberto de Muskam . Willelmo filio iohannis . Iohanne . de Mackesw’ . Ber
nardo de hawed’ . Willelmo filio meo et herede . Willelmo de Ritun cognato meo . Willelmo filio Alexandri . Et Multis aliis .
To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and future Walter of Ryedale son of Patrick of Ryedale sends greeting.

Know me to have granted and by this my charter to have confirmed to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and the monks serving God in that place for the souls of my lords, David, Malcolm, and William, kings of the Scots, and for the soul of my father and of all my ancestors and for the salvation of my lord, Alexander king of the Scots, for my own salvation and of all my successors, the donation of Patrick my father which he made to the same monks of a portion of his own land in the territory of Lilliesleaf, in free, pure, and everlasting alms, and is contained within these boundaries; that is, from the ford of Clarilawburn on the western side of 'Caldelawe', by the road that goes from Selkirk towards Jedburgh up to the Ale, and so descending by the same river which is the boundary between the land of Lilliesleaf and the bishop of Glasgow's land as far as the syke that is the boundary between the aforementioned land of Lilliesleaf and the abbot of Kelso, and so by the boundaries between the aforenamed land of Lilliesleaf and the same abbot as far as Clarilawburn and so upwards by the same burn as far as the aforesaid ford of Clarilawburn. This whole land and whatever is contained within these boundaries, except for the land that William son of Alexander holds in the same territory, I have granted to the said monks of Melrose; and I will and grant that they have, hold, and possess these things for ever, as freely, quitely, fully, and honourably as any alms freely, quitely, fully, and honourbaly hold and possess. In addition I have granted to the same monks pasture for twelve oxen, ten cows, fifteen horses and a hundred sheep, with all other common easements of the same toun, just as my father's charter of donation more fully shows and bears witness. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the aforesaid monks all things aforesaid in relation to all men and we shall discharge every earthly service, custom and secular exaction for ever in relation to the king and all others.

As witness, Earl Patrick, Patrick his son, Robert of Roos, Robert de Muschamp, William son of John, John ofMaxwell, Bernard of Howden, William my son and heir, my cousin William de Ritun, William son of Alexander, and many others.
